feed.title: AO3 - Evan "Buck" Buckley/Eddie Diaz (9-1-1 TV) (Filtered)
feed.subtitle: Latest works for Evan "Buck" Buckley/Eddie Diaz (9-1-1 TV) on Archive of Our Own [English, No ABO]

entry.title: Crosshairs at the park and The Things He Never Said
entry.summary:
Author: Wintersravens

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 3,491Chapters: 2/?Kudos: 62

Summary: Post-lawsuit, Buck goes for a run to clear his head and unexpectedly runs into Christopher and Tia Pepa at the park. When a sniper opens fire, Buck immediately protects them and helps get everyone to safety. During the chaos, he’s shot but hides his injury from Christopher and Pepa while continuing to keep them safe and help others around them.

  • Graphic Depictions Of Violence

entry.title: but i’m tied tight (cut me loose)
entry.summary:
Author: hereforthevibess

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 21,073Chapters: 3/4Kudos: 33

Summary: The atmosphere felt tense. He tried to ignore it. “I think I’m like — wasted,” Buck whispered. And Eddie knew he wasn’t. There was no sight of red-rimmed waterlines or blown out pupils, no high flushed cheekbones or sweaty forehead. He didn’t stumble drunkenly to the dorm, nor did he slur his words back on the porch of the party. Just like Eddie, he must be close to stone-cold sober. “Me too,” Eddie found himself saying, in an equally hushed tone. It felt like giving himself permission for something dangerous. — or, Eddie tries to navigate the complexities of his two most important relationships in college — Buck and Shannon.

  • Creator Chose Not To Use Archive Warnings

entry.title: man enough
entry.summary:
Author: coreliot

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 38,956Chapters: 7/40Kudos: 36

Summary: The world hits Eddie Diaz with sharp jabs; punches to the gut, chest, face. And he’s caught, trapped, between the blows—that are fast and hard like snipers to his left, like Greggs with a bullet in his chest, like Shannon bloody on the ground—and relief. Relief that comes quick and far between, like Christopher in his arms, like the 118 by his side, like a cold beer in his kitchen, just him and Buck. He’s always alternating between the two, never not ready for the blow, never not waiting for relief to end. That’s all he’s known, all he’s ever known. Or Eddie’s been the man of the house since he turned 11 years old. – Title from “Placing the Blame” by Self.

entry.title: it whispers, so listen closely
entry.summary:
Author: tristanthcrn

Rating: General AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 4,950Chapters: 1/1Kudos: 25

Summary: Two hours later, Buck is out of coloring books and stickers, and Theo has not stopped using him as a runway for the robot toy to launch off.  There are toys strewn across the living room and the kitchen, and now Theo is climbing on Buck like he’s a jungle gym.  The toddler squeals with delight, one arm draped over Buck’s shoulder as he plasters himself to the man’s back, toy whooshing through his curls and into the air.  “Theo, why don’t we sit down and watch a movie?” Buck offers, watching the toy as it sails through the air and falls to the floor just in front of the TV.  “I wanna play,” Theo insists, wrapping his arms around Buck’s neck and squeezing.“Theo, buddy, that’s too tight,” Buck gets out, gently—but firmly—prying the boy’s arms apart so he’s no longer being choked by a four-year-old.  “Gentle hands, okay?”“What’re gentle hands?”or, buck tries to placate theo with a movie. things don't go as planned. eddie comes to the rescue.

  • No Archive Warnings Apply

entry.title: my feet don't dance like they did with you
entry.summary:
Author: youcantsaveeveryone

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 10,844Chapters: 1/1Kudos: 13

Summary: From: [email protected] To: [email protected] Subject: Hi Hi Eddie, That seems almost too informal to capture the complicated mess of emotions involved in starting this email. How do you say hello to your (former?) best friend of 7 years who fundamentally changed you after almost 5 years of silence? I definitely don't know. So I'll start with hi. And I'm sorry. I'm sorry I let the silence stretch out. I'm sorry I let the gap between us widen. I'm sorry I let time slip by. It was days before I realized I hadn't replied your texts or answered your calls. And I let it turn into weeks, months, years. I don't want the silence to have lasted longer than our friendship.Or; 5 years after Eddie leaves LA again for El Paso, 5 years after he and Buck stop talking, Buck decides to send an email. (A story told through email correspondence)

entry.title: You Make It
entry.summary:
Author: gryff_grace

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 9,925Chapters: 2/?Kudos: 28

Summary: Welcome to the 118, Buck. I love you, kid. The words flicker across his mind as he drifts off to sleep. Bobby felt like home. And, even though Eddie won’t be in Buck’s bed tomorrow morning, even though Eddie’ll be squished between Chimney and Ravi on Buck’s living room floor and complaining about his aching back, even though Buck and Eddie won’t talk about this - the way they don’t talk about a lot of things - Eddie feels like home, too. * Or - Buck was once told, “You don’t find it, son. You make it.” Now, a year after Bobby’s death, that’s what Buck intends to do. With Eddie. Soon, they’ll both realize that making a life together is what they’ve been doing all along. A vision of what season 10 could be.

entry.title: S7 Superwoman: Last Responder
entry.summary:
Author: That_bitch_without_a_life

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 19,015Chapters: 6/7Kudos: 20

Summary: Having not dated anyone in years, and sick of waiting for Eddie to catch a clue, this year is about freedom and experiences for Kara, and maybe a chance to finally leave Krypton in the past. TLDR: this is a female buck fic if she was supergirl, just trust the process. (we're going to get rid of Tommy as soon as possible, this will be Buddie endgame, just not this season)

entry.title: Pops
entry.summary:
Author: Second_starr

Rating: General AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 2,037Chapters: 1/1Kudos: 127

Summary: Theo sits happily on the couch eating animal crackers. “Pops says Ravi screams during scary movies.” Ravi chokes on air. “How-” Theo gasps. “He says one time you cried.” Hen immediately loses it. Buck tries not to laugh and fails spectacularly. Ravi points accusingly at him. “You told him!” “I swear to god I didn’t!” Buck says, raising his hands in defense. Theo swings his legs cheerfully. “Pops said it was the dinosaur movie.” Ravi buries his face in his hands while Chim nearly falls off the couch laughing. Buck watches Theo carefully after that. Everything he says, leaving Buck slightly unsettled. Theo acts and is talking like someone is really there. Not imaginary-friend energy. Not make-believe. Real conversations. And every time Theo wanders off to talk to “Pops,” he comes back calmer. Happier. Safe. OR Theo has a new imaginary friend and it’s slowly freaking Buck out.

entry.title: mocker's jaw
entry.summary:
Author: dogfishmonger

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 4,909Chapters: 1/?Kudos: 7

Summary: He said, "It’s gonna be cold as shit here pretty soon. Just so you know."Eddie had flashed a tight smile that was maybe, if Buck was being generous, twenty percent genuine. Buck’s pretty sure he wasn’t supposed to see it; he only just glanced at it in the rearview by chance.Eddie gestured to the crystal-white sides of the trail they were navigating and the billow of steam from the exhaust pipe coming out behind them as the tires crunched through rapidly-hardening snow. "Think I got the memo on that one.""No," Buck said flatly, and cut his eyes from the mirror, "you haven’t. Not yet."—OR: An expedition into the mountains of Montana.—Title from Saintseneca's "Moon Barks at the Dog".

entry.title: Falling For You
entry.summary:
Author: chucks_prophet

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 1,216Chapters: 1/1Kudos: 59

Summary: Eddie’s been in the hospital many times, so none of this is unfamiliar. What is unfamiliar, however, is the man sitting at his bedside. The first distinguishing feature he notices is the birthmark just above his left eyebrow since he’s got his head bowed and cradled by his hand, arm resting on his chair. He’s asleep, that much is evident by the slow rise and fall of his broad chest.He clears his throat and the man startles awake. “You’re awake.”

entry.title: Everyone But Me
entry.summary:
Author: Smissy121

Rating: General AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 25,025Chapters: 8/18Kudos: 104

Summary: Everyone at the station knows the truth: Buck is the victim, and Eddie is the aggressor. The only problem? They’re completely wrong. As the 118 rallies to protect the man who is actually the architect of the abuse, the real victim is pushed further into the shadows. How do you escape when your only support system is actively silencing you? Author’s Note: Not Buck Friendly. Please read tags for warnings.

entry.title: hair of the dog, text of the drunk
entry.summary:
Author: madforyou

Rating: General AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 4,994Chapters: 1/1Kudos: 375

Summary: The phone lights up on the coffee table. It's a number he half-recognizes — a few weeks back a package for his apartment went to 4D by mistake, and he texted the guy to sort it out, and the guy wrote back a paragraph and a half about how the mail situation in this building was, his words, "a war crime." Eddie didn't save the number. He meant to. It just lives in his phone now as a stranger, and the stranger is texting him.Unknown: hey. HEY. ok this is the package guy from 4D. ur 4B. i need to ask u somethign and its importantUnknown: u have the good dog rightEddie: Who is this?Unknown: i JUST said. package guy. 4D. anyway i hear his little feet in the hallway sometiems. tap tap tap. like a tiny gentleman. and i need to know hes realEddie knows who this is. Big guy, loud, holds the elevator and then narrates the entire ride down.Eddie: Are you drunk?Unknown: thats a seperate issue from the dogWhich is not a no.Eddie: [image]Unknown: OH MY GODOr,Eddie gets a series of drunk texts from his hot neighbor.

entry.title: Postcards From LA
entry.summary:
Author: catniss_37

Rating: General AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 21,355Chapters: 9/11Kudos: 58

Summary: Eddie catches that redeye flight back to El Paso and returns to life in Texas with Christopher, and everything is as it should be. It's different and juggling Christopher, basic training, his grief is hard, and adapting to living with his parents right there to question his every move is complicated, but here with Christopher is where he's meant to be. Right? But when Eddie gets a postcard in the mail from one Evan "Buck" Buckley, suddenly he's reminded that maybe everything isn't as fine as it seems and that everything important isn't just here in Texas.

entry.title: good for u
entry.summary:
Author: popcorn_squirrel

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 1,740Chapters: 1/1Kudos: 128

Summary: Buck: hey!sonvm Eddie: ??? Buck: i said nvm Eddie: Yeah I know.I reject that. Buck: u can't reject my nvm??? Eddie: Sure can. What's up? or; Buck hasn't sexted in a while and he misses it. Eddie wants to help.

entry.title: Between Heaven and Hell: Buck’s Desire
entry.summary:
Author: caarmeeem

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 104,090Chapters: 85/?Kudos: 259

Summary: When the pact between the Buckleys and Lucifer Morningstar was sealed, Evan’s fate stopped being human. Born to save and be saved, he carries a divine gift that makes him light — but also forces him to face everything that light exposes. As a firefighter at the 118, Buck struggles with the emptiness that haunts him even on his brightest days. Until he meets Eddie Diaz and Christopher, two souls human enough to shatter everything he thought he knew about love, purpose, and belonging. But balance is fragile. Lucifer’s return to Earth unveils the bond between them and forces Buck to choose between the celestial calling that made him and the earthly love that completes him. Between heaven and hell, between the fire that destroys and the one that warms, Buck learns that salvation has a price — and that being light in a world afraid of it may be his greatest curse. A story about faith, family, and the weight of feeling everything — even what hurts. Where hell has a heart, heaven has consequences, and love might be the only form of redemption.

  • Creator Chose Not To Use Archive Warnings
  • Evan "Buck" Buckley/Eddie Diaz (9-1-1 TV)

entry.title: The Calling (It's a Choice)
entry.summary:
Author: Will_of_Alexander

Rating: General AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 20,833Chapters: 20/?Kudos: 309

Summary: Ever since the Diaz boys moved to El Paso, Buck has been estranged from their lives. Eddie first, and then Christopher. He's lived a lot since then. Gotten married, had a kid, gotten divorced. But the second his phone rings with the name "Chris 🦖" Buck knows something is wrong. Will he take the call? Will it become his calling? Does he even have a choice?
